Comment: Symmetric teardown of S3 cross-account replication (D-04). Reads the single ReplicationConfiguration on the source bucket, filters OUT the Rules belonging to the targeted Destinations[] (same deterministic ID convention as setup: 'repl-<DestAccountId>-<DestBucketBasename>'), then either re-puts the remaining Rules (PutBucketReplication) or deletes the whole config (DeleteBucketReplication) when none remain. Idempotent on not-found. Every S3 Task assumes the source role imperatively via Credentials.RoleArn.$.

GetReplicationConfig.Comment: Read the existing ReplicationConfiguration. GetBucketReplication throws S3.ReplicationConfigurationNotFoundError ONLY when no config exists (Pitfall 1); that is an idempotent no-op for teardown -> NoReplicationExists. Any OTHER error (throttle/AccessDenied/transient) MUST route to DeleteFailed (CR-02) so a failed teardown is never reported as a no-op success.

FilterRules.Comment: Compute the set of Rule IDs to remove from Destinations[] using the SAME deterministic convention as setup ('repl-<DestAccountId>-<DestBucketBasename>' truncated to 255 chars), filter the existing Rules to drop those IDs, and expose remainingRules + remainingEmpty.

CheckRemainingEmpty.Comment: If no rule remains after filtering, delete the whole config; otherwise re-put the remaining Rules.

DeleteAllReplication.Comment: No rule remains: remove the entire ReplicationConfiguration from the source bucket.

PutRemainingConfig.Comment: Rules remain for other spokes: write back the filtered Rules array (PutBucketReplication replaces the whole config, so the full remaining set is written).

Parameters.Message: Targeted destinations' replication rules removed from the source bucket.
No replication configuration found on the source bucket; teardown is a no-op.

DeleteFailed.Cause: Failed to remove S3 cross-account replication rules from the source bucket.
